content:
## Add support for image insertion on Android

This PR adds support for image keyboard support on Android, utilizing the commitContent function.

Partially fixes flutter/flutter#20796. Associated engine PR: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/35619.

This is a re-PR of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/87203 to clean up the commits.

cc @justinmc, @hixie, and @zlshames.

## Pre-launch Checklist

- [x] I read the [Contributor Guide] and followed the process outlined there for submitting PRs.
- [x] I read the [Tree Hygiene] wiki page, which explains my responsibilities.
- [x] I read and followed the [Flutter Style Guide], including [Features we expect every widget to implement].
- [x] I signed the [CLA].
- [x] I listed at least one issue that this PR fixes in the description above.
- [x] I updated/added relevant documentation (doc comments with `///`).
- [x] I added new tests to check the change I am making or feature I am adding, or Hixie said the PR is test-exempt.
- [x] All existing and new tests are passing.

~~@justinmc what can I do about this ci.yaml validation? It says required but is failing.... I didn't change anything for any ci.yaml stuff~~

Following the instructions and merging upstream did the trick 😄
@justinmc I got all checks to pass, this should be ready for a review now :)

:mega: We’re building smart automated test selection to slash your CI/CD build times. [Learn more](https://about.codecov.io/iterative-testing/?utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=flutter)
@justinmc what is next to get this moving forward? The only potential blocker I see right now is that test you wanted me to write, which I talked about in your most recent review.
@tneotia I just commented with an idea about the test, let me know if that helps or if you still can't get it going!

> @tneotia I just commented with an idea about the test, let me know if that helps or if you still can't get it going!

Can't believe I was missing such a small detail 😅 - it works now!!
@justinmc do you know why the bot thinks this PR affects goldens?

It seems to be ok, the [goldens comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/110052#issuecomment-1307258601) above appears out of date and doesn't show any broken goldens.  I've removed the "will affect goldens" label.
This looks good to go except for the Google testing failure.  I'm actually struggling to see what the problem is there.  I'm asking around about it and will post back.

The google testing is failing because it's introducing a new method to the `TextInputClient` interface which some of the internal classes implement so unfortunately I think this is a [breaking change](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Tree-hygiene#1-determine-if-your-change-is-a-breaking-change).  @tneotia would you mind adding a migration guide (and maybe a dartfix) following these steps in the wiki? https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Tree-hygiene#3-prepare-your-change
@LongCatIsLooong there is already a migration PR created at https://github.com/flutter/website/pull/7692 by request from @justinmc :)
Ah good to know. I'll start migrating the internal clients once the migration guide PR is merged.
@LongCatIsLooong should I mark that migration PR as ready to review then? Or leave it as draft?
@tneotia can you rebase this PR with tip of tree? It's 50 commits behind which is causing issues with the Google testing infra.
@CaseyHillers done, although Google testing is likely broken due to the breaking change on `TextInputClient`

> Ah good to know. I'll start migrating the internal clients once the migration guide PR is merged.

It looks like the migration guide PR https://github.com/flutter/website/pull/7692 won't be merged until this PR lands (because the migration guide needs to say when the implementation landed.)

Fortunately, from the latest review https://github.com/flutter/website/pull/7692#pullrequestreview-1233203821 on that migration guide PR, it seems like it's ready except for minor nits and that information about when this PR landed.

So: @LongCatIsLooong, perhaps that draft is ready for you to use to start migrating the Google-internal clients?

fwiw, we've been using the merged engine code in our production app with Flutter 3.7 and this PR cherry-picked in (as per https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/20796#issuecomment-1406933421). Still working great and would be absolutely amazing to have this merged in officially!
The analyzer check is failing, likely because of dart 3.0 nnbd changes. Could you take a look?

> The analyzer check is failing, likely because of dart 3.0 nnbd changes. Could you take a look?

Should be good now :)
@justinmc or @LongCatIsLooong the Google Testing failures have several breakages. Can you prep the internal patch before landing this PR?

@CaseyHillers Done, pinging you the CL.

@tneotia I think the 2 other failures are infra flakes, but I'm not able to rerun them... Maybe push a merge commit?
@justinmc looks like all tests are a go now (apart from Google testing, not sure if that is already fixed or not)
Awesome, thank you!  The google tests should be fixed on our side soon if not already.  We might have to manually set it to passing.
@tneotia Could you sync this to the tip of the tree?
@LongCatIsLooong done :)
Thanks @tneotia for your persistence with this PR!  A lot of people are going to appreciate this work.

No problem. Glad we could contribute this feature to Flutter, definitely a must-have for chat apps!

Hoepfully someone can pick up the torch and add iOS / Web / Desktop support to this API (right now we use the [pasteboard](https://pub.dev/packages/pasteboard) package for web & desktop).

Regardless, thanks for sticking with us as well for this! Super excited for this to land. When should I reopen the migration guide PR?
@tneotia Ah good call, I'll reopen it now.

## Pan and zoom gallery demo Adding a demo to the flutter_gallery app that shows how to do some basic canvas operations like panning, pinch to zoom, and hit detection. Relevant issues: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/8272, https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/12878 This PR won't close those issues, but after this goes in I'll open a new PR to move my code into a reusable widget that is more like what people are asking for.

TODO:

  - [x] Bring back the hex grid
  - [x] Add tap to select hex tile
  - [x] Zoom on the point where the user's fingers are
  - [x] Rotation
  - [x] Rotation around an arbitrary point?
The test failures seem to be due to a bug in the engine that I'm hoping to fix here: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/8247

You can make the State private and grab it via "dynamic". If you make the
State public you are committing to that as a public API.

On Mon, Apr 1, 2019, 14:29 Justin McCandless <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> *@justinmc* commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In
> examples/flutter_gallery/lib/demo/pan_and_zoom_demo_transform_interaction.dart
> <https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/25164#discussion_r271058126>:
>
> > +  final bool disableScale;
> +  final bool disableRotation;
> +  final Offset initialTranslation;
> +  final double initialScale;
> +  final double initialRotation;
> +
> +  @override TransformInteractionState createState() => TransformInteractionState();
> +}
> +
> +enum GestureType {

> +  scale,
> +  rotate,
> +}
> +
> +class TransformInteractionState extends State<TransformInteraction> with TickerProviderStateMixin {
>

> test. Is that valid idea or is there a better way?
